title: S3 Objects, Keys, Listings, and Runtime Shapes · Type-System Trace: Key Prefix Design
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing typed S3 object-model ledger; use opaque application identifiers, readonly object records, paginator async iteration, and runtime validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the key prefix design increment in typed S3 object-model ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, design opaque or structured keys with explicit delimiter, normalization, tenant, cardinality, migration, and disclosure rules.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish bucket, key, version ID, delete marker, upload ID, part number, and application identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Key Prefix Design · Theory Key Prefix Design
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1607 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate runtime-valid object key, version, pagination, and strong-read evidence; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, design opaque or structured keys with explicit delimiter, normalization, tenant, cardinality, migration, and disclosure rules.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Build Key Prefix Design evidence · Theory Key Prefix Design
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-key-prefix-design-76cx9r" that uses case 7804 to separate runtime-valid object key, version, pagination, and strong-read evidence. Concept boundary: contrast one accepted path, one near miss, and the claim that still needs transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
